pages.text:
Raychem Heat-Shrink Joint up to 72.5 kV
The Raychem Heat-Shrink Joint EHVS
The joint is based on a heat-shrink
design for voltage classes up to 72.5 kV.
Polymeric insulated cables of various
designs can be adapted with respect
to shielding and metal sheath. Our
heat-shrink accessories have been
used by utilities and industrial compa-
nies around the world for more than
35 years. This ongoing field experience
has made TE Connectivity a leader in
materials science and technology
for high-voltage applications. The
TE Energy materials technology is at
the core of the development of our
heat shrink joints. The materials used
in our cable accessories have been
extensively optimized with respect to
product design and function, manufac-
turing and expected service environ-
ments.Furthermore the system is
suitable for paper and polymeric cable
constructions and is also designed
to accommodate modern jointing Expanded
requirements such as mechanical
shear bolt connectors.
Heat-Shrink Technology
The conductive outer layer (1) together electrical interfaces and a perfect
with the insulating middle layer (2) seal against moisture ingress. The
represents the heat shrinkable hold out elastomeric properties of the inner
for the inner elastomeric layer (3) of the insulation layer of the joint body allow
joint body. During the shrinking process for any cable diameter compensation
the stored recovery force of the elas- and adjustment resulting from the load 1 2 3
tomeric layer is released and adds up to cycling of the cable. At the same time
the recovery force generated by the the two outer heat shrinkable layers Recovered
heat shrinkable outer layers of the joint provide a tight and rigid belt for the
body. The resulting high compression joint body representing a high level of
forces as well as the perfect ability of mechanical protection against outer
geometrical adjustment provides tight environmental influences.
Cable shielding Size transition Cable insulation Connector
Grounding kits for commonly The size transition connector The joint is suitable to handle The mechanical connector
used cable constructions like is able to link cables with cables with different insula- with torque controlled shear-
lead sheath, corrugated alu- different conductor and/or tion layers like XLPE, EPR or off bolts is suitable for all
minum sheath, copper tape, insulation diameter. Paper MIND. common conductors made
wire screens and combina- out of aluminium or copper.
tions are available. A special connector construc-
tion allows also the jointing of
cables with small insulation
thickness.

images.context_text: Raychem Heat-Shrink Joint up to 72.5 kV The Raychem Heat-Shrink Joint EHVS The joint is based on a heat-shrink design for voltage classes up to 72.5 kV. Polymeric insulated cables of various designs can be adapted with respect to shielding and metal sheath. Our heat-shrink accessories have been used by utilities and industrial compa- nies around the world for more than 35 years. This ongoing field experience has made TE Connectivity a leader in materials science and technology for high-voltage applications. The TE Energy materials technology is at the core of the development of our heat shrink joints. The materials used in our cable accessories have been extensively optimized with respect to product design and function, manufac- turing and expected service environ- ments.Furthermore the system is suitable for paper and polymeric cable constructions and is also designed to accommodate modern jointing Expanded requirements such as mechanical shear bolt connectors. Heat-Shrink Technology The conductive outer layer (1) together electrical interfaces and a perfect with the insulating middle layer (2) seal against moisture ingress. The represents the heat shrinkable hold out elastomeric properties of the inner for the inner elastomeric layer (3) of the insulation layer of the joint body allow joint body. During the shrinking process for any cable diameter compensation the stored recovery force of the elas- and adjustment resulting from the load 1 2 3 tomeric layer is released and adds up to cycling of the cable. At the same time the recovery force generated by the the two outer heat shrinkable layers Recovered heat shrinkable outer layers of the joint provide a tight and rigid belt for the body. The resulting high compression joint body representing a high level of forces as well as the perfect ability of mechanical protection against outer geometrical adjustment provides tight environmental influences. Cable shielding Size transition Cable insulation Connector Grounding kits for commonly The size transition connector The joint is suitable to handle The mechanical connector used cable constructions like is able to link cables with cables with different insula- with torque controlled shear- lead sheath, corrugated alu- different conductor and/or tion layers like XLPE, EPR or off bolts is suitable for all minum sheath, copper tape, insulation diameter. Paper MIND. common conductors made wire screens and combina- out of aluminium or copper. tions are available. A

pages.text:
Raychem Heat-Shrink Joint up to 72.5 kV
Shield connectivity Mechanical connector Outer joint protection
Solderless connections are used to The mechanical connector is designed A heat-shrinkable tube with a strong
connect the metallic –shields, -sheaths for all common conductor types, made wall thickness and internally pre-coated
or –armours of the cables. The compo- of aluminium or copper. with adhesive, provides the outer pro-
nents continue the correct cross section Bolts with a predetermined shear-off tection and a lasting moisture sealing.
across the connection area, even for torque ensure reliable contact and easy
different metallic layers. In addition a handling at every installation.
layer of copper mesh is applied around
the joint, to assure safe contact and
protection.
1 2 3
Electrical field control Shield continuity
The stress control tube (1) has a pre- and the inner wall of the screened tube Shield break, straight through and
cisely defined impedance characteristic (3) restore the conductor insulation. grounding connections are possible.
which smoothes the electrical field The elastic memory of the heat-shrink
over the connector and cable screen components ensures the correct wall
ends. High voltage insulating tube (2) thickness is obtained.

pages.text:
Raychem Heat-Shrink Joint up to 72.5 kV
Technical data: 52 kV 72.5 kV
Relevant standards IEC 60840 / IEEE 404
Rated voltage U /U (U ) kV 26/46 (52) 36/66 (72.5)
0 m
Basic impulse level kV 250 350
Max. continuous operating temperature °C 90 90
Max. conductor emergency temperature °C 130 130
Conductor short circuit temperature °C 250 250
Short circuit current (sheath) kA / 1sec 10 10
For shield break joints:
DC voltage between metallic
sheaths/screens kV 20 20
DC voltage between metallic sheath/screen
and earthed exterior kV 20 20
Lightning impulse voltage between
metallic sheath/screen kV 60 60
Lightning impulse voltage between metallic
sheath/screen and earthed exterior kV 30 30
Application Range:
Conductor mm2 1600 1600
Diameter over insulation mm 30 – 86 30 – 86
Diameter over jacket mm 100 100
For special applications and bigger cable sizes please contact your TE Energy representative.
Tools
Tools required for cable preparation can also be purchased or rented from TE Energy.
(See brochure EPP-0756 and EPP-1543)
Cable stripper Gas torch Straightening slide rails Tool box Heating blanket
